You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.175 (commit: 05a5e229).

## Issue to Resolve

[BUG]: Edit Note Preview does not show the expected card
### Checked for duplicates?

- [X] This issue is not a duplicate

### What are the steps to reproduce this bug?

open the browser
Select a card which is not card 1 of the note
long press
in the menu, tap "edit note"
Tap preview

### Expected behaviour

You should see the selected card in the preview mode (or at least, the card should be consistent with the card underlined at the bottom of the editor)

### Actual behaviour

First card of the note is shown (At least I assume. In the case I was interested in, I saw the "this card is empty" warning)

### Debug info

```text
2.17 alpha 3
```


### (Optional) Anything else you want to share?

_No response_

### Research

- [X] I am reporting a bug specific to AnkiDroid (Android app)
- [X] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue
- [ ] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting))

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

This will be slightly easier to fix once https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/pull/14719 goes in:

* `ordinal` is provided to the editor: 
* `ordinal` is `ord`, NOT the index into the array
  * `{{c1::AA}} {{c3::BB}}` returns the second ordinal as 2

https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/blob/170c2d1ee5a354b42395e8c0de4363a1e1fc367b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplatePreviewer.kt#L84

`mOrdinal` is not used to set `templateIndex` in this branch:

https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/blob/170c2d1ee5a354b42395e8c0de4363a1e1fc367b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplatePreviewer.kt#L233-L241

`setCurrentCardFromNoteEditorBundle` uses `templateIndex = 0` and displays the wrong card

I've added two regression tests to help guide a fix

One undefined question: what should the note editor previewer show if one of the cards is empty? Should it be skipped, or displayed

Should this be different between:
* Card Template Preview
* Note Editor Preview
* Card Browser Preview (less likely to want to see empty cards)

For when a few dependencies have been merged:

```kotlin
    private fun indexFromOrdinal(col: Collection, ordinal: Int): Int {
        return when (mEditedNotetype?.isCloze) {
            true -> col.clozeNumbersInNote(col.newNote(mEditedNotetype!!)).indexOf(mOrdinal)
            else -> ordinal
        }
    }
```
Is that correct? with c1 and c3, that will return [1,3], and ord 3 will be an index out of bounds, won't it?

Edit: sorry, ignore me - I mistook this for numbers[ord] instead of searching for the given value.
In the note editor preview, there seems to be no specific card. So I don’t understand what sense it would make to have this behavior on the note editor preview.

For card template preview, I certainly believe you should see the preview for the card you are looking it.

For the browser, if the card selected in the browser is empty, I believe you should show that the card is empty. That’s an information I certainly may want to have if it is the case. Because either:
* there was no recent "clean card"
* there was a clear card, and this is card 1, generated in order to ensure the note is not deleted. 

The first case may be interesting, the second is certainly something that should be noted.

Also, if we touch this part of the code, it may be worth to give more context, by stating either:
* This card is empty. The n cards of this note are empty.
* This card is empty. Jump to the first non-empty card. (with a link/button)

And I guess, potentially, a checkbox to skip the empty card when browsing which cards will be generated.


If you agree it makes sense, I assume it should be many different bugs so they can be easily tackled

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplatePreviewer.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/Collection.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

462→    data class TimeboxReached(val secs: Int, val reps: Int)
463→
464→    /* Return (elapsedTime, reps) if timebox reached, or null.
465→    * Automatically restarts timebox if expired. */
466→    fun timeboxReached(): TimeboxReached? {
467→        if (sched.timeboxSecs() == 0) {
468→            // timeboxing disabled
469→            return null
470→        }
471→        val elapsed = TimeManager.time.intTime() - mStartTime
472→        val limit = sched.timeboxSecs()
473→        return if (elapsed > limit) {
474→            TimeboxReached(
475→                limit,
476→                sched.reps - mStartReps
477→            ).also {
478→                startTimebox()
479→            }
480→        } else {
481→            null
482→        }
483→    }

548→    @KotlinCleanup("have getIds() return a list of mids and define idsToStr over it")
549→    fun basicCheck(): Boolean {
550→        // cards without notes
551→        if (db.queryScalar("select 1 from cards where nid not in (select id from notes) limit 1") > 0) {
552→            return false
553→        }
554→        val badNotes = db.queryScalar(
555→            "select 1 from notes where id not in (select distinct nid from cards) " +
556→                "or mid not in " + Utils.ids2str(notetypes.ids()) + " limit 1"
557→        ) > 0
558→        // notes without cards or models
559→        if (badNotes) {
560→            return false
561→        }
562→        // invalid ords
563→        for (m in notetypes.all()) {
564→            // ignore clozes
565→            if (m.getInt("type") != Consts.MODEL_STD) {
566→                continue
567→            }
568→            // Make a list of valid ords for this model
569→            val tmpls = m.getJSONArray("tmpls")
570→            val badOrd = db.queryScalar(
571→                "select 1 from cards where (ord < 0 or ord >= ?) and nid in ( " +
572→                    "select id from notes where mid = ?) limit 1",
573→                tmpls.length(),
574→                m.getLong("id")
575→            ) > 0
576→            if (badOrd) {
577→                return false
578→            }
579→        }
580→        return true
581→    }

583→    fun log(vararg objects: Any?) {
584→        if (!debugLog) return
585→
586→        val unixTime = TimeManager.time.intTime()
587→
588→        val outerTraceElement = Thread.currentThread().stackTrace[3]
589→        val fileName = outerTraceElement.fileName
590→        val methodName = outerTraceElement.methodName
591→
592→        val objectsString = objects
593→            .map { if (it is LongArray) Arrays.toString(it) else it }
594→            .joinToString(", ")
595→
596→        writeLog("[$unixTime] $fileName:$methodName() $objectsString")
597→    }

599→    private fun writeLog(s: String) {
600→        mLogHnd?.let {
601→            try {
602→                it.println(s)
603→            } catch (e: Exception) {
604→                Timber.w(e, "Failed to write to collection log")
605→            }
606→        }
607→        Timber.d(s)
608→    }

610→    private fun _openLog() {
611→        Timber.i("Opening Collection Log")
612→        if (!debugLog) {
613→            return
614→        }
615→        try {
616→            val lpath = File(path.replaceFirst("\\.anki2$".toRegex(), ".log"))
617→            if (lpath.exists() && lpath.length() > 10 * 1024 * 1024) {
618→                val lpath2 = File("$lpath.old")
619→                if (lpath2.exists()) {
620→                    lpath2.delete()
621→                }
622→                lpath.renameTo(lpath2)
623→            }
624→            mLogHnd = PrintWriter(BufferedWriter(FileWriter(lpath, true)), true)
625→        } catch (e: IOException) {
626→            // turn off logging if we can't open the log file
627→            Timber.e("Failed to open collection.log file - disabling logging")
628→            debugLog = false
629→        }
630→    }
631→
632→    private fun _closeLog() {
633→        Timber.i("Closing Collection Log")
634→        mLogHnd?.close()
635→        mLogHnd = null
636→    }

 34→/** A wrapper for a notetype in JSON format with helpers for editing the notetype. */
 35→class CardTemplateNotetype(val notetype: NotetypeJson) {
 36→    enum class ChangeType {
 37→        ADD, DELETE
 38→    }
 39→
 40→    private var mTemplateChanges = ArrayList<Array<Any>>()
 41→    var editedModelFileName: String? = null
 42→
 43→    fun toBundle(): Bundle = bundleOf(
 44→        INTENT_MODEL_FILENAME to saveTempModel(AnkiDroidApp.instance.applicationContext, notetype),
 45→        "mTemplateChanges" to mTemplateChanges
 46→    )
 47→
 48→    private fun loadTemplateChanges(bundle: Bundle) {
 49→        try {
 50→            mTemplateChanges = bundle.getSerializableCompat("mTemplateChanges")!!
 51→        } catch (e: ClassCastException) {
 52→            Timber.e(e, "Unexpected cast failure")
 53→        }
 54→    }
 55→
 56→    fun getTemplate(ord: Int): JSONObject {
 57→        Timber.d("getTemplate() on ordinal %s", ord)
 58→        return notetype.getJSONArray("tmpls").getJSONObject(ord)
 59→    }
 60→
 61→    val templateCount: Int
 62→        get() = notetype.getJSONArray("tmpls").length()
 63→
 64→    val modelId: NoteTypeId
 65→        get() = notetype.getLong("id")
 66→
 67→    fun updateCss(css: String?) {
 68→        notetype.put("css", css)
 69→    }
 70→
 71→    val css: String
 72→        get() = notetype.getString("css")
 73→
 74→    fun updateTemplate(ordinal: Int, template: JSONObject) {
 75→        notetype.getJSONArray("tmpls").put(ordinal, template)
 76→    }
 77→
 78→    fun addNewTemplate(newTemplate: JSONObject) {
 79→        Timber.d("addNewTemplate()")
 80→        addTemplateChange(ChangeType.ADD, newTemplate.getInt("ord"))
 81→    }
 82→
 83→    fun removeTemplate(ord: Int) {
 84→        Timber.d("removeTemplate() on ordinal %s", ord)
 85→        addTemplateChange(ChangeType.DELETE, ord)
 86→    }
 87→
 88→    fun saveToDatabase(collection: com.ichi2.libanki.Collection) {
 89→        Timber.d("saveToDatabase() called")
 90→        dumpChanges()
 91→        clearTempModelFiles()
 92→        return saveModel(collection, notetype, adjustedTemplateChanges)
 93→    }
 94→
 95→    /**
 96→     * Template deletes shift card ordinals in the database. To operate without saving, we must keep track to apply in order.
 97→     * In addition, we don't want to persist a template add just to delete it later, so we combine those if they happen
 98→     */
 99→    fun addTemplateChange(type: ChangeType, ordinal: Int) {
100→        Timber.d("addTemplateChange() type %s for ordinal %s", type, ordinal)
101→        val templateChanges = templateChanges
102→        val change = arrayOf<Any>(ordinal, type)
103→
104→        // If we are deleting something we added but have not saved, edit it out of the change list
105→        if (type == ChangeType.DELETE) {
106→            var ordinalAdjustment = 0
107→            for (i in templateChanges.indices.reversed()) {
108→                val oldChange = templateChanges[i]
109→                when (oldChange[1] as ChangeType) {
110→                    ChangeType.DELETE -> if (oldChange[0] as Int - ordinalAdjustment <= ordinal) {
111→                        // Deleting an ordinal at or below us? Adjust our comparison basis...
112→                        ordinalAdjustment++
113→                        continue
114→                    }
115→                    ChangeType.ADD -> if (ordinal == oldChange[0] as Int - ordinalAdjustment) {
116→                        // Deleting something we added this session? Edit it out via compaction
117→                        compactTemplateChanges(oldChange[0] as Int)
118→                        return
119→                    }
120→                }
121→            }
122→        }
123→        Timber.d("addTemplateChange() added ord/type: %s/%s", change[0], change[1])
124→        templateChanges.add(change)
125→        dumpChanges()
126→    }
127→
128→    /**
129→     * Return an int[] containing the collection-relative ordinals of all the currently pending deletes,
130→     * including the ordinal passed in, as opposed to the changelist-relative ordinals
131→     *
132→     * @param ord int UI-relative ordinal to check database for delete safety along with existing deletes
133→     * @return int[] of all ordinals currently in the database, pending delete
134→     */
135→    fun getDeleteDbOrds(ord: Int): IntArray {
136→        dumpChanges()
137→        Timber.d("getDeleteDbOrds()")
138→
139→        // array containing the original / db-relative ordinals for all pending deletes plus the proposed one
140→        val deletedDbOrds = ArrayList<Int>(mTemplateChanges.size)
141→
142→        // For each entry in the changes list - and the proposed delete - scan for deletes to get original ordinal
143→        for (i in 0..mTemplateChanges.size) {
144→            var ordinalAdjustment = 0
145→
146→            // We need an initializer. Though proposed change is checked last, it's a reasonable default initializer.
147→            var currentChange = arrayOf<Any>(ord, ChangeType.DELETE)
148→            if (i < mTemplateChanges.size) {
149→                // Until we exhaust the pending change list we will use them
150→                currentChange = mTemplateChanges[i]
151→            }
152→
153→            // If the current pending change isn't a delete, it is unimportant here
154→            if (currentChange[1] !== ChangeType.DELETE) {
155→                continue
156→            }
157→
158→            // If it is a delete, scan previous deletes and shift as necessary for original ord
159→            for (j in 0 until i) {
160→                val previousChange = mTemplateChanges[j]
161→
162→                // Is previous change a delete? Lower ordinal than current change?
163→                if (previousChange[1] === ChangeType.DELETE && previousChange[0] as Int <= currentChange[0] as Int) {
164→                    // If so, that is the case where things shift. It means our ordinals moved and original ord is higher
165→                    ordinalAdjustment++
166→                }
167→            }
168→
169→            // We know how many times ordinals smaller than the current were deleted so we have the total adjustment
170→            // Save this pending delete at it's original / db-relative position
171→            deletedDbOrds.add(currentChange[0] as Int + ordinalAdjustment)
172→        }
173→        val deletedDbOrdInts = IntArray(deletedDbOrds.size)
174→        for (i in deletedDbOrdInts.indices) {
175→            deletedDbOrdInts[i] = deletedDbOrds[i]
176→        }
177→        return deletedDbOrdInts
178→    }
179→
180→    private fun dumpChanges() {
181→        if (!BuildConfig.DEBUG) {
182→            return
183→        }
184→        val adjustedChanges = adjustedTemplateChanges
185→        for (i in mTemplateChanges.indices) {
186→            val change = mTemplateChanges[i]
187→            val adjustedChange = adjustedChanges[i]
188→            Timber.d("dumpChanges() Change %s is ord/type %s/%s", i, change[0], change[1])
189→            Timber.d(
190→                "dumpChanges() During save change %s will be ord/type %s/%s",
191→                i,
192→                adjustedChange[0],
193→                adjustedChange[1]
194→            )
195→        }
196→    }
197→
198→    val templateChanges: ArrayList<Array<Any>>
199→        get() {
200→            return mTemplateChanges
201→        }
202→
203→    /**
204→     * Adjust the ordinals in our accrued change list so that any pending adds have the correct
205→     * ordinal after taking into account any pending deletes
206→     *
207→     * @return ArrayList<Object></Object>[2]> of [ordinal][ChangeType] entries
208→     */
209→    val adjustedTemplateChanges: ArrayList<Array<Any>>
210→        get() {
211→            val changes = templateChanges
212→            val adjustedChanges = ArrayList<Array<Any>>(changes.size)
213→
214→            // In order to save the changes into the database, the ordinals in the changelist must correspond to the
215→            // ordinals in the database (for deletes) or the correct index in the changes array (for adds)
216→            // It is not possible to know what those will be until the user requests a save, so they are stored in the
217→            // change list as-is until the save time comes, then the adjustment is made all at once
218→            for (i in changes.indices) {
219→                val change = changes[i]
220→                val adjustedChange = arrayOf(change[0], change[1])
221→                when (adjustedChange[1] as ChangeType) {
222→                    ChangeType.ADD -> {
223→                        adjustedChange[0] = getAdjustedAddOrdinalAtChangeIndex(this, i)
224→                        Timber.d(
225→                            "getAdjustedTemplateChanges() change %s ordinal adjusted from %s to %s",
226→                            i,
227→                            change[0],
228→                            adjustedChange[0]
229→                        )
230→                    }
231→                    ChangeType.DELETE -> {}
232→                }
233→                adjustedChanges.add(adjustedChange)
234→            }
235→            return adjustedChanges
236→        }
237→
238→    /**
239→     * Scan the sequence of template add/deletes, looking for the given ordinal.
240→     * When found, purge that ordinal and shift future changes down if they had ordinals higher than the one purged
241→     */
242→    private fun compactTemplateChanges(addedOrdinalToDelete: Int) {
243→        Timber.d(
244→            "compactTemplateChanges() merge/purge add/delete ordinal added as %s",
245→            addedOrdinalToDelete
246→        )
247→        var postChange = false
248→        var ordinalAdjustment = 0
249→        var i = 0
250→        while (i < mTemplateChanges.size) {
251→            val change = mTemplateChanges[i]
252→            var ordinal = change[0] as Int
253→            val changeType = change[1] as ChangeType
254→            Timber.d("compactTemplateChanges() examining change entry %s / %s", ordinal, changeType)
255→
256→            // Only make adjustments after the ordinal we want to delete was added
257→            if (!postChange) {
258→                if (ordinal == addedOrdinalToDelete && changeType == ChangeType.ADD) {
259→                    Timber.d("compactTemplateChanges() found our entry at index %s", i)
260→                    // Remove this entry to start compaction, then fix up the loop counter since we altered size
261→                    postChange = true
262→                    mTemplateChanges.removeAt(i)
263→                    i--
264→                }
265→                i++
266→                continue
267→            }
268→
269→            // We compact all deletes with higher ordinals, so any delete is below us: shift our comparison basis
270→            if (changeType == ChangeType.DELETE) {
271→                ordinalAdjustment++
272→                Timber.d(
273→                    "compactTemplateChanges() delete affecting purged template, shifting basis, adj: %s",
274→                    ordinalAdjustment
275→                )
276→            }
277→
278→            // If following ordinals were higher, we move them as part of compaction
279→            if (ordinal + ordinalAdjustment > addedOrdinalToDelete) {
280→                Timber.d("compactTemplateChanges() shifting later/higher ordinal down")
281→                change[0] = --ordinal
282→            }
283→            i++
284→        }
285→    }
286→
287→    companion object {
288→        const val INTENT_MODEL_FILENAME = "editedModelFilename"
289→
290→        /**
291→         * Load the TemporaryModel from the filename included in a Bundle
292→         *
293→         * @param bundle a Bundle that should contain persisted JSON under INTENT_MODEL_FILENAME key
294→         * @return re-hydrated TemporaryModel or null if there was a problem, null means should reload from database
295→         */
296→        fun fromBundle(bundle: Bundle): CardTemplateNotetype? {
297→            val editedModelFileName = bundle.getString(INTENT_MODEL_FILENAME)
298→            // Bundle.getString is @Nullable, so we have to check.
299→            if (editedModelFileName == null) {
300→                Timber.d("fromBundle() - model file name under key %s", INTENT_MODEL_FILENAME)
301→                return null
302→            }
303→            Timber.d("onCreate() loading saved model file %s", editedModelFileName)
304→            val tempNotetypeJSON: NotetypeJson = try {
305→                getTempModel(editedModelFileName)
306→            } catch (e: IOException) {
307→                Timber.w(e, "Unable to load saved model file")
308→                return null
309→            }
310→            val model = CardTemplateNotetype(tempNotetypeJSON)
311→            model.loadTemplateChanges(bundle)
312→            return model
313→        }
314→
315→        /**
316→         * Save the current model to a temp file in the application internal cache directory
317→         * @return String representing the absolute path of the saved file, or null if there was a problem
318→         */
319→        fun saveTempModel(context: Context, tempModel: JSONObject): String? {
320→            Timber.d("saveTempModel() saving tempModel")
321→            var tempModelFile: File
322→            try {
323→                ByteArrayInputStream(tempModel.toString().toByteArray()).use { source ->
324→                    tempModelFile = File.createTempFile("editedTemplate", ".json", context.cacheDir)
325→                    compat.copyFile(source, tempModelFile.absolutePath)
326→                }
327→            } catch (ioe: IOException) {
328→                Timber.e(ioe, "Unable to create+write temp file for model")
329→                return null
330→            }
331→            return tempModelFile.absolutePath
332→        }
333→
334→        /**
335→         * Get the model temporarily saved into the file represented by the given path
336→         * @return JSONObject holding the model, or null if there was a problem
337→         */
338→        @Throws(IOException::class)
339→        fun getTempModel(tempModelFileName: String): NotetypeJson {
340→            Timber.d("getTempModel() fetching tempModel %s", tempModelFileName)
341→            try {
342→                ByteArrayOutputStream().use { target ->
343→                    compat.copyFile(tempModelFileName, target)
344→                    return NotetypeJson(target.toString())
345→                }
346→            } catch (e: IOException) {
347→                Timber.e(e, "Unable to read+parse tempModel from file %s", tempModelFileName)
348→                throw e
349→            }
350→        }
351→
352→        /** Clear any temp model files saved into internal cache directory  */
353→        fun clearTempModelFiles(): Int {
354→            var deleteCount = 0
355→            for (c in AnkiDroidApp.instance.cacheDir.listFiles() ?: arrayOf()) {
356→                val absolutePath = c.absolutePath
357→                if (absolutePath.contains("editedTemplate") && absolutePath.endsWith("json")) {
358→                    if (!c.delete()) {
359→                        Timber.w("Unable to delete temp file %s", c.absolutePath)
360→                    } else {
361→                        deleteCount++
362→                        Timber.d("Deleted temp model file %s", c.absolutePath)
363→                    }
364→                }
365→            }
366→            return deleteCount
367→        }
368→
369→        /**
370→         * Check if the given ordinal from the current UI state (which includes all pending changes) is a pending add
371→         *
372→         * @param ord int representing an ordinal in the model, that might be an unsaved addition
373→         * @return boolean true if it is a pending addition from this editing session
374→         */
375→        fun isOrdinalPendingAdd(model: CardTemplateNotetype, ord: Int): Boolean {
376→            for (i in model.templateChanges.indices) {
377→                // commented out to make the code compile, why is this unused?
378→                // val change = model.templateChanges[i]
379→                val adjustedOrdinal = getAdjustedAddOrdinalAtChangeIndex(model, i)
380→                if (adjustedOrdinal == ord) {
381→                    Timber.d(
382→                        "isOrdinalPendingAdd() found ord %s was pending add (would adjust to %s)",
383→                        ord,
384→                        adjustedOrdinal
385→                    )
386→                    return true
387→                }
388→            }
389→            Timber.d("isOrdinalPendingAdd() ord %s is not a pending add", ord)
390→            return false
391→        }
392→
393→        /**
394→         * Check if the change at the given index in the changes array is an addition from this editing session
395→         * (and thus is not in the database yet, and possibly needing ordinal adjustment from subsequent deletes)
396→         * @param changesIndex the index of the template in the changes array
397→         * @return either ordinal adjusted by any pending deletes if it is a pending add, or -1 if the ordinal is not an add
398→         */
399→        fun getAdjustedAddOrdinalAtChangeIndex(model: CardTemplateNotetype, changesIndex: Int): Int {
400→            if (changesIndex >= model.templateChanges.size) {
401→                return -1
402→            }
403→            var ordinalAdjustment = 0
404→            val change = model.templateChanges[changesIndex]
405→            val ordinalToInspect = change[0] as Int
406→            for (i in model.templateChanges.size - 1 downTo changesIndex) {
407→                val oldChange = model.templateChanges[i]
408→                val currentOrdinal = change[0] as Int
409→                when (oldChange[1] as ChangeType) {
410→                    ChangeType.DELETE -> {
411→                        // Deleting an ordinal at or below us? Adjust our comparison basis...
412→                        if (currentOrdinal - ordinalAdjustment <= ordinalToInspect) {
413→                            ordinalAdjustment++
414→                            continue
415→                        }
416→                        Timber.d(
417→                            "getAdjustedAddOrdinalAtChangeIndex() contemplating delete at index %s, current ord adj %s",
418→                            i,
419→                            ordinalAdjustment
420→                        )
421→                    }
422→                    ChangeType.ADD -> if (changesIndex == i) {
423→                        // something we added this session
424→                        Timber.d(
425→                            "getAdjustedAddOrdinalAtChangeIndex() pending add found at at index %s, old ord/adjusted ord %s/%s",
426→                            i,
427→                            currentOrdinal,
428→                            currentOrdinal - ordinalAdjustment
429→                        )
430→                        return currentOrdinal - ordinalAdjustment
431→                    }
432→                }
433→            }
434→            Timber.d(
435→                "getAdjustedAddOrdinalAtChangeIndex() determined changesIndex %s was not a pending add",
436→                changesIndex
437→            )
438→            return -1
439→        }
440→    }
441→}

 36→/**
 37→ * A Card is the ultimate entity subject to review; it encapsulates the scheduling parameters (from which to derive
 38→ * the next interval), the note it is derived from (from which field data is retrieved), its own ownership (which deck it
 39→ * currently belongs to), and the retrieval of presentation elements (filled-in templates).
 40→ *
 41→ * Card presentation has two components: the question (front) side and the answer (back) side. The presentation of the
 42→ * card is derived from the template of the card's Card Type. The Card Type is a component of the Note Type (see Models)
 43→ * that this card is derived from.
 44→ *
 45→ * This class is responsible for:
 46→ * - Storing and retrieving database entries that map to Cards in the Collection
 47→ * - Providing the HTML representation of the Card's question and answer
 48→ * - Recording the results of review (answer chosen, time taken, etc)
 49→ *

 55→ * Queue: same as above, and:

 57→ * Due is used differently for different queues.

content.text:
The issue was that when the Note Editor Preview was opened, it always defaulted to showing the first card of the note, regardless of which card was currently selected or being edited. This was because the `cardIndex` (the index in the list of templates/clozes) was initialized to 0 and not updated based on the `mOrdinal` (the card's `ord`) passed from the `NoteEditor`.

I have implemented a fix in `CardTemplatePreviewer.kt` that:
1.  Calculates the correct `cardIndex` from the provided `mOrdinal` when the previewer is launched from the Note Editor. For standard notes, this is a direct mapping, while for cloze notes, it finds the index of the cloze ordinal in the list of generated clozes.
2.  Adds robustness to `numberOfCardsEphemeral` and `cardIndexToOrd` to handle cases where a cloze note might not have any cloze markers, ensuring at least one card is generated and preventing potential crashes.

These changes ensure that the previewer shows the card consistent with the one being edited or selected in the browser.

Summary of changes:
- Modified `CardTemplatePreviewer.onCollectionLoaded` to initialize `cardIndex` using a new `indexFromOrdinal` helper method when `mNoteEditorBundle` is present.
- Added `indexFromOrdinal` private method to handle the mapping from card `ord` to `cardIndex`.
- Updated `Note.numberOfCardsEphemeral` to ensure it returns at least 1 for cloze notes.
- Updated `Note.cardIndexToOrd` to safely handle empty cloze number lists.

No linter errors were introduced by these changes.

260→    /** Mark schema modified to force a full sync.
261→     * ConfirmModSchemaException will be thrown if the user needs to be prompted to confirm the action.
262→     * If the user chooses to confirm then modSchemaNoCheck should be called, after which the exception can
263→     * be safely ignored, and the outer code called again.
264→     *
265→     * @throws ConfirmModSchemaException
266→     */
267→    @Throws(ConfirmModSchemaException::class)
268→    fun modSchema() {
269→        if (!schemaChanged()) {
270→            /* In Android we can't show a dialog which blocks the main UI thread
271→             Therefore we can't wait for the user to confirm if they want to do
272→             a full sync here, and we instead throw an exception asking the outer
273→             code to handle the user's choice */
274→            throw ConfirmModSchemaException()
275→        }
276→        modSchemaNoCheck()
277→    }

457→    fun startTimebox() {
458→        mStartTime = TimeManager.time.intTime()
459→        mStartReps = sched.reps

461→
462→    data class TimeboxReached(val secs: Int, val reps: Int)
463→
464→    /* Return (elapsedTime, reps) if timebox reached, or null.
465→    * Automatically restarts timebox if expired. */
466→    fun timeboxReached(): TimeboxReached? {
467→        if (sched.timeboxSecs() == 0) {
468→            // timeboxing disabled
469→            return null
470→        }
471→        val elapsed = TimeManager.time.intTime() - mStartTime
472→        val limit = sched.timeboxSecs()
473→        return if (elapsed > limit) {
474→            TimeboxReached(
475→                limit,
476→                sched.reps - mStartReps
477→            ).also {
478→                startTimebox()
479→            }
480→        } else {
481→            null
482→        }
483→    }

545→    /*
546→     * Basic integrity check. Only used by unit tests.
547→     */
548→    @KotlinCleanup("have getIds() return a list of mids and define idsToStr over it")
549→    fun basicCheck(): Boolean {
550→        // cards without notes
551→        if (db.queryScalar("select 1 from cards where nid not in (select id from notes) limit 1") > 0) {
552→            return false
553→        }
554→        val badNotes = db.queryScalar(
555→            "select 1 from notes where id not in (select distinct nid from cards) " +
556→                "or mid not in " + Utils.ids2str(notetypes.ids()) + " limit 1"
557→        ) > 0
558→        // notes without cards or models
559→        if (badNotes) {
560→            return false
561→        }
562→        // invalid ords
563→        for (m in notetypes.all()) {
564→            // ignore clozes
565→            if (m.getInt("type") != Consts.MODEL_STD) {
566→                continue
567→            }
568→            // Make a list of valid ords for this model
569→            val tmpls = m.getJSONArray("tmpls")
570→            val badOrd = db.queryScalar(
571→                "select 1 from cards where (ord < 0 or ord >= ?) and nid in ( " +
572→                    "select id from notes where mid = ?) limit 1",
573→                tmpls.length(),
574→                m.getLong("id")
575→            ) > 0
576→            if (badOrd) {
577→                return false
578→            }
579→        }
580→        return true
581→    }

583→    fun log(vararg objects: Any?) {
584→        if (!debugLog) return
585→
586→        val unixTime = TimeManager.time.intTime()
587→
588→        val outerTraceElement = Thread.currentThread().stackTrace[3]
589→        val fileName = outerTraceElement.fileName
590→        val methodName = outerTraceElement.methodName
591→
592→        val objectsString = objects
593→            .map { if (it is LongArray) Arrays.toString(it) else it }
594→            .joinToString(", ")
595→
596→        writeLog("[$unixTime] $fileName:$methodName() $objectsString")
597→    }
598→
599→    private fun writeLog(s: String) {
600→        mLogHnd?.let {
601→            try {
602→                it.println(s)
603→            } catch (e: Exception) {
604→                Timber.w(e, "Failed to write to collection log")
605→            }
606→        }
607→        Timber.d(s)
608→    }
609→
610→    private fun _openLog() {
611→        Timber.i("Opening Collection Log")
612→        if (!debugLog) {
613→            return
614→        }
615→        try {
616→            val lpath = File(path.replaceFirst("\\.anki2$".toRegex(), ".log"))
617→            if (lpath.exists() && lpath.length() > 10 * 1024 * 1024) {
618→                val lpath2 = File("$lpath.old")
619→                if (lpath2.exists()) {
620→                    lpath2.delete()
621→                }
622→                lpath.renameTo(lpath2)
623→            }
624→            mLogHnd = PrintWriter(BufferedWriter(FileWriter(lpath, true)), true)
625→        } catch (e: IOException) {
626→            // turn off logging if we can't open the log file
627→            Timber.e("Failed to open collection.log file - disabling logging")
628→            debugLog = false
629→        }
630→    }
631→
632→    private fun _closeLog() {
633→        Timber.i("Closing Collection Log")
634→        mLogHnd?.close()
635→        mLogHnd = null
636→    }

 34→/** A wrapper for a notetype in JSON format with helpers for editing the notetype. */
 35→class CardTemplateNotetype(val notetype: NotetypeJson) {
 36→    enum class ChangeType {
 37→        ADD, DELETE
 38→    }
 39→
 40→    private var mTemplateChanges = ArrayList<Array<Any>>()
 41→    var editedModelFileName: String? = null
 42→
 43→    fun toBundle(): Bundle = bundleOf(
 44→        INTENT_MODEL_FILENAME to saveTempModel(AnkiDroidApp.instance.applicationContext, notetype),
 45→        "mTemplateChanges" to mTemplateChanges
 46→    )
 47→
 48→    private fun loadTemplateChanges(bundle: Bundle) {
 49→        try {
 50→            mTemplateChanges = bundle.getSerializableCompat("mTemplateChanges")!!
 51→        } catch (e: ClassCastException) {
 52→            Timber.e(e, "Unexpected cast failure")
 53→        }
 54→    }
 55→
 56→    fun getTemplate(ord: Int): JSONObject {
 57→        Timber.d("getTemplate() on ordinal %s", ord)
 58→        return notetype.getJSONArray("tmpls").getJSONObject(ord)
 59→    }
 60→
 61→    val templateCount: Int
 62→        get() = notetype.getJSONArray("tmpls").length()
 63→
 64→    val modelId: NoteTypeId
 65→        get() = notetype.getLong("id")
 66→
 67→    fun updateCss(css: String?) {
 68→        notetype.put("css", css)
 69→    }
 70→
 71→    val css: String
 72→        get() = notetype.getString("css")
 73→
 74→    fun updateTemplate(ordinal: Int, template: JSONObject) {
 75→        notetype.getJSONArray("tmpls").put(ordinal, template)
 76→    }
 77→
 78→    fun addNewTemplate(newTemplate: JSONObject) {
 79→        Timber.d("addNewTemplate()")
 80→        addTemplateChange(ChangeType.ADD, newTemplate.getInt("ord"))
 81→    }
 82→
 83→    fun removeTemplate(ord: Int) {
 84→        Timber.d("removeTemplate() on ordinal %s", ord)
 85→        addTemplateChange(ChangeType.DELETE, ord)
 86→    }
 87→
 88→    fun saveToDatabase(collection: com.ichi2.libanki.Collection) {
 89→        Timber.d("saveToDatabase() called")
 90→        dumpChanges()
 91→        clearTempModelFiles()
 92→        return saveModel(collection, notetype, adjustedTemplateChanges)
 93→    }
 94→
 95→    /**
 96→     * Template deletes shift card ordinals in the database. To operate without saving, we must keep track to apply in order.
 97→     * In addition, we don't want to persist a template add just to delete it later, so we combine those if they happen
 98→     */
 99→    fun addTemplateChange(type: ChangeType, ordinal: Int) {
100→        Timber.d("addTemplateChange() type %s for ordinal %s", type, ordinal)
101→        val templateChanges = templateChanges
102→        val change = arrayOf<Any>(ordinal, type)
103→
104→        // If we are deleting something we added but have not saved, edit it out of the change list
105→        if (type == ChangeType.DELETE) {
106→            var ordinalAdjustment = 0
107→            for (i in templateChanges.indices.reversed()) {
108→                val oldChange = templateChanges[i]
109→                when (oldChange[1] as ChangeType) {
110→                    ChangeType.DELETE -> if (oldChange[0] as Int - ordinalAdjustment <= ordinal) {
111→                        // Deleting an ordinal at or below us? Adjust our comparison basis...
112→                        ordinalAdjustment++
113→                        continue
114→                    }
115→                    ChangeType.ADD -> if (ordinal == oldChange[0] as Int - ordinalAdjustment) {
116→                        // Deleting something we added this session? Edit it out via compaction
117→                        compactTemplateChanges(oldChange[0] as Int)
118→                        return
119→                    }
120→                }
121→            }
122→        }
123→        Timber.d("addTemplateChange() added ord/type: %s/%s", change[0], change[1])
124→        templateChanges.add(change)
125→        dumpChanges()

128→    /**
129→     * Return an int[] containing the collection-relative ordinals of all the currently pending deletes,
130→     * including the ordinal passed in, as opposed to the changelist-relative ordinals
131→     *
132→     * @param ord int UI-relative ordinal to check database for delete safety along with existing deletes
133→     * @return int[] of all ordinals currently in the database, pending delete
134→     */
135→    fun getDeleteDbOrds(ord: Int): IntArray {
136→        dumpChanges()
137→        Timber.d("getDeleteDbOrds()")
138→
139→        // array containing the original / db-relative ordinals for all pending deletes plus the proposed one
140→        val deletedDbOrds = ArrayList<Int>(mTemplateChanges.size)
141→
142→        // For each entry in the changes list - and the proposed delete - scan for deletes to get original ordinal
143→        for (i in 0..mTemplateChanges.size) {
144→            var ordinalAdjustment = 0
145→
146→            // We need an initializer. Though proposed change is checked last, it's a reasonable default initializer.
147→            var currentChange = arrayOf<Any>(ord, ChangeType.DELETE)
148→            if (i < mTemplateChanges.size) {
149→                // Until we exhaust the pending change list we will use them
150→                currentChange = mTemplateChanges[i]
151→            }
152→
153→            // If the current pending change isn't a delete, it is unimportant here
154→            if (currentChange[1] !== ChangeType.DELETE) {
155→                continue
156→            }
157→
158→            // If it is a delete, scan previous deletes and shift as necessary for original ord
159→            for (j in 0 until i) {
160→                val previousChange = mTemplateChanges[j]
161→
162→                // Is previous change a delete? Lower ordinal than current change?
163→                if (previousChange[1] === ChangeType.DELETE && previousChange[0] as Int <= currentChange[0] as Int) {
164→                    // If so, that is the case where things shift. It means our ordinals moved and original ord is higher
165→                    ordinalAdjustment++
166→                }
167→            }
168→
169→            // We know how many times ordinals smaller than the current were deleted so we have the total adjustment
170→            // Save this pending delete at it's original / db-relative position
171→            deletedDbOrds.add(currentChange[0] as Int + ordinalAdjustment)
172→        }
173→        val deletedDbOrdInts = IntArray(deletedDbOrds.size)
174→        for (i in deletedDbOrdInts.indices) {
175→            deletedDbOrdInts[i] = deletedDbOrds[i]
176→        }
177→        return deletedDbOrdInts
178→    }

203→    /**
204→     * Adjust the ordinals in our accrued change list so that any pending adds have the correct
205→     * ordinal after taking into account any pending deletes
206→     *
207→     * @return ArrayList<Object></Object>[2]> of [ordinal][ChangeType] entries
208→     */
209→    val adjustedTemplateChanges: ArrayList<Array<Any>>
210→        get() {
211→            val changes = templateChanges
212→            val adjustedChanges = ArrayList<Array<Any>>(changes.size)
213→
214→            // In order to save the changes into the database, the ordinals in the changelist must correspond to the
215→            // ordinals in the database (for deletes) or the correct index in the changes array (for adds)
216→            // It is not possible to know what those will be until the user requests a save, so they are stored in the
217→            // change list as-is until the save time comes, then the adjustment is made all at once
218→            for (i in changes.indices) {
219→                val change = changes[i]
220→                val adjustedChange = arrayOf(change[0], change[1])
221→                when (adjustedChange[1] as ChangeType) {
222→                    ChangeType.ADD -> {
223→                        adjustedChange[0] = getAdjustedAddOrdinalAtChangeIndex(this, i)
224→                        Timber.d(
225→                            "getAdjustedTemplateChanges() change %s ordinal adjusted from %s to %s",
226→                            i,
227→                            change[0],
228→                            adjustedChange[0]
229→                        )
230→                    }
231→                    ChangeType.DELETE -> {}
232→                }
233→                adjustedChanges.add(adjustedChange)
234→            }
235→            return adjustedChanges
236→        }

238→    /**
239→     * Scan the sequence of template add/deletes, looking for the given ordinal.
240→     * When found, purge that ordinal and shift future changes down if they had ordinals higher than the one purged
241→     */
242→    private fun compactTemplateChanges(addedOrdinalToDelete: Int) {
243→        Timber.d(
244→            "compactTemplateChanges() merge/purge add/delete ordinal added as %s",
245→            addedOrdinalToDelete
246→        )
247→        var postChange = false
248→        var ordinalAdjustment = 0
249→        var i = 0
250→        while (i < mTemplateChanges.size) {
251→            val change = mTemplateChanges[i]
252→            var ordinal = change[0] as Int
253→            val changeType = change[1] as ChangeType
254→            Timber.d("compactTemplateChanges() examining change entry %s / %s", ordinal, changeType)
255→
256→            // Only make adjustments after the ordinal we want to delete was added
257→            if (!postChange) {
258→                if (ordinal == addedOrdinalToDelete && changeType == ChangeType.ADD) {
259→                    Timber.d("compactTemplateChanges() found our entry at index %s", i)
260→                    // Remove this entry to start compaction, then fix up the loop counter since we altered size
261→                    postChange = true
262→                    mTemplateChanges.removeAt(i)
263→                    i--
264→                }
265→                i++
266→                continue
267→            }
268→
269→            // We compact all deletes with higher ordinals, so any delete is below us: shift our comparison basis
270→            if (changeType == ChangeType.DELETE) {
271→                ordinalAdjustment++
272→                Timber.d(
273→                    "compactTemplateChanges() delete affecting purged template, shifting basis, adj: %s",
274→                    ordinalAdjustment
275→                )
276→            }
277→
278→            // If following ordinals were higher, we move them as part of compaction
279→            if (ordinal + ordinalAdjustment > addedOrdinalToDelete) {
280→                Timber.d("compactTemplateChanges() shifting later/higher ordinal down")
281→                change[0] = --ordinal
282→            }
283→            i++
284→        }
285→    }

373→         * @return boolean true if it is a pending addition from this editing session
374→         */
375→        fun isOrdinalPendingAdd(model: CardTemplateNotetype, ord: Int): Boolean {
376→            for (i in model.templateChanges.indices) {

378→                // val change = model.templateChanges[i]
379→                val adjustedOrdinal = getAdjustedAddOrdinalAtChangeIndex(model, i)
380→                if (adjustedOrdinal == ord) {

393→        /**
394→         * Check if the change at the given index in the changes array is an addition from this editing session
395→         * (and thus is not in the database yet, and possibly needing ordinal adjustment from subsequent deletes)
396→         * @param changesIndex the index of the template in the changes array
397→         * @return either ordinal adjusted by any pending deletes if it is a pending add, or -1 if the ordinal is not an add
398→         */
399→        fun getAdjustedAddOrdinalAtChangeIndex(model: CardTemplateNotetype, changesIndex: Int): Int {
400→            if (changesIndex >= model.templateChanges.size) {
401→                return -1
402→            }
403→            var ordinalAdjustment = 0
404→            val change = model.templateChanges[changesIndex]
405→            val ordinalToInspect = change[0] as Int
406→            for (i in model.templateChanges.size - 1 downTo changesIndex) {
407→                val oldChange = model.templateChanges[i]
408→                val currentOrdinal = change[0] as Int
409→                when (oldChange[1] as ChangeType) {
410→                    ChangeType.DELETE -> {
411→                        // Deleting an ordinal at or below us? Adjust our comparison basis...
412→                        if (currentOrdinal - ordinalAdjustment <= ordinalToInspect) {
413→                            ordinalAdjustment++
414→                            continue
415→                        }
416→                        Timber.d(
417→                            "getAdjustedAddOrdinalAtChangeIndex() contemplating delete at index %s, current ord adj %s",
418→                            i,
419→                            ordinalAdjustment
420→                        )
421→                    }
422→                    ChangeType.ADD -> if (changesIndex == i) {
423→                        // something we added this session
424→                        Timber.d(
425→                            "getAdjustedAddOrdinalAtChangeIndex() pending add found at at index %s, old ord/adjusted ord %s/%s",
426→                            i,
427→                            currentOrdinal,
428→                            currentOrdinal - ordinalAdjustment
429→                        )
430→                        return currentOrdinal - ordinalAdjustment
431→                    }
432→                }
433→            }
434→            Timber.d(
435→                "getAdjustedAddOrdinalAtChangeIndex() determined changesIndex %s was not a pending add",
436→                changesIndex
437→            )
438→            return -1
439→        }
440→    }
